title: Civil Aviation (Amendment) Act 2026
text:
REPUBLIC OF NAURU 
CIVIL AVIATION (AMENDMENT) ACT 2026 
_____________________________ 
No. 12 of 2026 
______________________________ 
 
An Act to amend the Civil Aviation Act 2011 and for other related purposes. 
 
Certified: 26 March 2026 
Table of Provisions 
1 Short title ....................................................................................................................................... 2 
2 Commencement ............................................................................................................................ 2 
3 Amendment of the Civil Aviation Act 2011 .................................................................................... 2 
4 Amendment of Section 6 ............................................................................................................... 2 
5 Amendment of Section 87 ............................................................................................................. 2 
6 General amendment to the Act ..................................................................................................... 2

2 
 
Enacted by the Parliament of Nauru as follows: 
1 Short title 
This Act may be cited as the Civil Aviation (Amendment) Act 2026. 
2 Commencement 
This Act commences on the date it is certified by the Speaker. 
3 Amendment of the Civil Aviation Act 2011 
The Civil Aviation Act 2011 is amended by the provisions of this Act. 
4 Amendment of Section 6 
Section 6 is amended as follows: 
(a) by inserting the following new definitions in its correct alphabetical order: 
 
‘‘e-cigarette’ has the meaning given to it under the Tobacco Control Act 
2009; 
 
‘tobacco product’ has the meaning given to it under the Tobacco Control 
Act 2009; and’; and 
 
(b) by deleting the definition of ‘smoke’ and substituting it with the following: 
‘‘smoke’ or ‘smoking’ means: 
 
(a) puffing or inhaling and expelling the smoke of any tobacco product 
or using, holding of or control over any ignited tobacco product; or 
 
(b) use, activate or inhaling from an e-cigarette;’. 
5 Amendment of Section 87 
Section 87 is amended by substituting with the following: 
'87 Designated officers 
The Chief Commissioner may designate a person to be a designated officer 
for an investigation.’. 
6 General amendment to the Act 
All references to the term ‘authorised officer’ throughout the Act are deleted and 
substituted with ‘designated officer’.
